item.title: It?s called wage theft.
item.description: Woo that ended up longer than I?d planned. Recently I was told I have a terrible work ethic because I don?t believe that you need to turn up for work early to set up for your shift. My job requires me to log into my pc and start up and log into my tools and they expect me to do that before my shift starts. Nope. I don?t do that for fun or pleasure, therefore it is work, therefore, pay me for my time accordingly. Petty? Probably but this is how people get taken advantage of. (originally posted by @Chocolatecoveredmoose on 2021-06-12 21:07:42)
item.dc:creator: ThisTimeLastYear

item.title: How to Adult {UPDATED}
item.description:
**FP Edit: this post made me Glorious! Thank you for the accolades!!
This is actually a sales tactic used to create rapport with customers. So, unless you can do this naturally somehow (not 3 times in 5 minutes), this would be a definite turn off if I just met you in a social situation. Just, take this one with a grain of salt. Don't be too aggressive about it.

item.title: Perfect coordination!!!, this is called having blind trust in a person
item.dc:creator: maureoko

item.title: In honour of Pride Month, here's my rollercoaster of a weekend
item.description: I'm so happy and can't stop smiling! Don't know how I'm going to get anything done in the next few days, all I'm thinking about is her. Can't wait for Wednesday!!!! After being married to a man for 9 years, divorce, losing my job, and a bunch of other shit last year, I feel like I haven't felt in years. It also helps that she is drop dead gorgeous, and I can't believe she thinks the same about me! How anyone can look like an angel while hungover is beyond me. With a few girls I tried dating or just hooked up with, I felt like they were afraid or ashamed (of me or themselves, idk). This one kissed me in the street, held my hand, kissed me goodbye at the bus stop, didn't want to let go. We live in a pretty homophobic country, so she was so brave and made me feel safe to do that. She's just amazing, I don't know what else to say! EDIT: After five years on Imgur, my first post to reach MV! Send memes to send to her I guess? No nudes please :)
item.dc:creator: jelisaveta

item.title: So Punks have invaded an German island that is inhabited by upper class people
item.description: Guy with the ice cream is watching them like animals in the zoo Edit: rich people made some condescending comments before the 9€ ticket (you can travel with every public transport for 9€ per month in germany at the moment) and it became a meme to go to Sylt, an island were rich people have summer houses. And now a big amount of punks got there and get drunk the entire day. They even ordered beer from amazon and had it delivered to the packing station so they wouldn't have to carry it.
item.dc:creator: uebermacht

item.title: My journey to good mental health and well-being
item.description:
This is me in 2017. I was 46 and 25 stone (350lbs/159kgs at 5'10/178cm). I was really unhappy. I remember that day really well. I felt incredibly self-conscious and full of self-loathing and shame. I was trying to enjoy a day out with my lovely son but I was only too aware of some of the judgmental glances I got from other diners which tainted my enjoyment. At the time I was sharing a home with my ex-husband. Looking back I don't know why I agreed to live like this. But my ex told me we couldn't afford for us to live separately so I went along with his plan. This had been my problem. Not having my own voice. Not having the confidence to live my life as I wished. My married had been unhappy because of this imbalance of power. But I reached a limit and realised that I could just move out anyway. There was a lot of gaslighting from my ex. This took the form of him saying I would be a huge financial burden to him as I couldn't manage my own finances as I was a "spendthrift". But eventually I moved out anyway. I started renting my own place 3 years ago almost to the day. I had the good fortune to rent a lovely semi-detached cottage on a farm by a beautiful forest that leads down to a often deserted beach. Having my own space with access to beautiful outdoor space gave me the tools I needed to start improving my mental health and addressing the issues that were either the cause of, or the symptoms of, my mental health issues. I started to walk a lot. Miles and miles in the woods. It took some time to find the mental strength to address my comfort eating but last year I underwent a lifestyle overhaul that really changed everything. I cut out sugar and carbs and ate healthily. My weight started to go down and my mental health improved. This year I got a part-time job and in April I started doing crossfit 3 times a week. I haven't felt this well and calm in own skin in my whole life. I like who I see in the mirror. I'm proud of my strong, fit body. Which is getting stronger and fitter every week. I've lost weight before but this time I made the focus health (mental and physical) and well-being rather than being as skinny as possible. That change of focus changed the game completely. At 51 I've never felt better and I'm excited for the future. To any other unhappy middle-aged people out there find what motivates you and make that first step to changing your life. It's never too late to change everything.
From a size 26 to a size 16 ?
Me now Thank you so so much to all the lovely imgurians who have commented on my post. I love this community so much. I have been inspired by many stories shared on this site and I hope my story inspires others to turn that shit around and turn it into pure fucking gold. xxx
item.dc:creator: CazzaMcSpazza
